# Security Policy

<a id="toc"></a>
## Table of Contents

- [Reporting Vulnerabilities](#reporting-vulnerabilities)
- [Do Not Put Sensitive Detail in Public Issues](#do-not-put-sensitive-detail-in-public-issues)
- [Required Evidence](#required-evidence)

<a id="reporting-vulnerabilities"></a>
## Reporting Vulnerabilities

Use GitHub private vulnerability reporting when available. Otherwise contact the repository security owner through the approved private channel.

<a id="do-not-put-sensitive-detail-in-public-issues"></a>
## Do Not Put Sensitive Detail in Public Issues

Do not disclose exploit steps, credentials, tokens, private keys, customer data, production endpoints, or unreleased vulnerability detail in public issues, pull requests, logs, tests, fixtures, or documentation.

<a id="required-evidence"></a>
## Required Evidence

Security-sensitive fixes require a linked security review, affected-control mapping, regression test, release record, and vulnerability response notes before release.
